risks.description: The Ø7 h6, Ø9 x6, and Ø6 k6 journals are small precision fit diameters on a case-hardened shaft; they are achievable but likely require post-heat-treatment OD grinding/lapping and careful inspection.
risks.mitigation: Control heat-treatment distortion, leave grinding allowance, use OD grinding/lapping and verify with the shop's dimensional inspection capability.

risks.description: The helical gear includes gauge-over-teeth tolerance of 0/-0.019 mm, two-flank working error 12 µm, radial composite error 32 µm, and runout to A-B; these are within the shop's gear grinding/honing/inspection capability but sensitive to case-hardening distortion.
risks.mitigation: Use in-house gear finishing after heat treatment where required, auto-straightening if needed, and gear measurement verification.

risks.description: The active gear axial extent, Ø10/Ø11 interpretation, surface texture applicability, and relief groove depths are not fully resolved in the extracted feature graph.
risks.mitigation: Confirm the listed clarifications before final routing or quotation; feasibility is not blocked because all implicated processes are available in-house.

feasibility.assessment_notes: The part is a small cylindrical case-hardened helical gear shaft, 61.4 mm long by Ø11.79 mm maximum, well within the CNC lathe, sliding-head, VMC, grinding, and gear-machine envelopes. Required in-house capabilities are present: turning, center drilling, hobbing/shaping, gear shaving/grinding/honing, OD grinding/lapping, sealed-quench case hardening, straightening, deburring, and inspection.

clarifications.question: Confirm the exact axial extent of the active helical tooth face: should the gear feature length be treated as the 46.5 ±0.1 mm span, the 23/23.5 mm segmented chain, or the 20.9 mm dimension shown in the lower view?
clarifications.why_it_matters: This affects gear face-width/profile-length interpretation, but not basic shop capability.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use 46.5 ±0.1 mm as the overall geared/shaft span with 20.9 mm recorded as visible/profile tooth length until clarified.

clarifications.question: Confirm whether the Ø10 and Ø11 callouts are separate turned cylindrical steps or gear/root-related reference diameters within the toothed zone.
clarifications.why_it_matters: This affects feature definition for F006/F007, but both turning and gear processes are available.
clarifications.suggested_default: Model Ø10 as a coaxial intermediate gear-end shaft step and keep Ø11/Ø11.79 as gear-related diameters.

clarifications.question: Confirm the applicability of the Rz 6 and Rz 2.5 surface texture symbols to the gear flanks, Ø9 x6 shaft, and Ø6 k6 journal surfaces.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Exact leader applicability affects which surfaces require final grinding/lapping or gear finishing.
clarifications.suggested_default: Apply Rz 6 to the gear tooth zone and Rz 2.5 to the datum/bearing journal surfaces as extracted.

clarifications.question: Confirm the intended interpretation of DIN 332-R2x4.25 and DIN 332-R1.6x3 centre-hole dimensions, specifically whether the second value is the required axial depth/length for the centre hole.
clarifications.why_it_matters: DIN 332 notation may define standard geometry rather than a directly toleranced depth, though either interpretation is manufacturable.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat R2x4.25 and R1.6x3 as centre-hole standard designations with depth/length values 4.25 mm and 3.0 mm respectively.

clarifications.question: What is the depth/profile of F005 left annular relief groove?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The width is manufacturable, but the missing groove depth/profile prevents complete feature definition.

clarifications.question: What is the depth/profile of F009 right shoulder relief / transition before Ø6 k6 journal?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The extracted width, angle, and chamfer are manufacturable, but the missing profile/depth prevents complete feature definition.

assumptions.text: 16MnCrS5 1.7139 is treated as an alloy case-hardening steel within the shop's alloy_steel material capability.
The shop's OD grinding/lapping and gear grinding/honing capabilities may be used for final precision after case hardening; no outside process is permitted or needed.
ISO fit classes h6, x6, and k6 are interpreted as tight IT6-class journal requirements; exact upper/lower deviations should be confirmed from ISO 286 during detailed planning.
Surface texture callouts Rz 6 and Rz 2.5 are assessed using the extracted feature applicability; exact drawing leader applicability remains a clarification item.

reasoning_summary: This is a small case-hardened 16MnCrS5 helical gear shaft that fits easily within the shop's machine envelopes and uses processes the shop has in-house, including gear cutting/finishing, OD grinding/lapping, sealed-quench heat treatment, straightening, and inspection. The part can proceed, but with medium risk due to precision fit-class journals, gear accuracy after heat treatment, and several drawing interpretation ambiguities around gear length, surface texture applicability, and relief groove depths.
